solution: 
```cpp
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <map>
#include <set>
#include <unordered_map>

using namespace std;

class FoodRatings {
public:
    FoodRatings(vector<string>& foods, vector<string>& cuisines, vector<int>& ratings) {
        for (int i = 0; i < foods.size(); ++i) {
            string& food = foods[i];
            string& cuisine = cuisines[i];
            int rating = ratings[i];
            foodToCuisine[food] = cuisine;
            foodToRating[food] = rating;
            cuisineMap[cuisine].insert({-rating, food}); // Use negative rating for max-heap behavior
        }
    }

    void changeRating(string food, int newRating) {
        string cuisine = foodToCuisine[food];
        int oldRating = foodToRating[food];
        // Remove the old entry
        auto& setRef = cuisineMap[cuisine];
        setRef.erase({-oldRating, food});
        // Update the rating
        foodToRating[food] = newRating;
        // Add the new entry
        setRef.insert({-newRating, food});
    }

    string highestRated(string cuisine) {
        auto& setRef = cuisineMap[cuisine];
        return (*setRef.begin()).second;
    }

private:
    unordered_map<string, string> foodToCuisine;
    unordered_map<string, int> foodToRating;
    map<string, set<pair<int, string>>> cuisineMap;
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n) for changeRating and highestRated
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a combination of maps and sets to efficiently manage and query the highest-rated food items for each cuisine. The `foodToCuisine` and `foodToRating` maps allow O(1) access to the cuisine and rating of a food item. The `cuisineMap` stores a set of pairs (negative rating, food name) for each cuisine, enabling O(log n) operations for updating and retrieving the highest-rated food. The use of negative ratings ensures the set behaves like a max-heap. This approach efficiently handles the constraints and operations required.
